name: Tyropitta (Cheese Triangles)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the 3 tablespoons butter in a saucepan. Add the flour and stir well to combine.
2. Gradually add the hot milk, stirring constantly until the mixture is thick and creamy. Remove from heat.
3. Stir in the salt and pepper until incorporated. Let cool slightly.
4. Crumble the feta cheese into small pieces and add it to the sauce.
5. Add the beaten eggs; stir well, then incorporate the dill and mix until thoroughly combined.
6. Cut each phyllo sheet lengthwise into 3 strips. Brush one strip at a time with melted butter, keeping the others covered to prevent drying.
7. Place a teaspoon of filling in the lower left corner of the phyllo strip. Fold over to form a triangle along the strip.
8. Fold the upper left corner over to seal the triangle. Continue folding the phyllo strips until the entire strip forms a small triangle (about 3 inches).
9. Brush the top with butter and repeat the process for all triangles.
10. Arrange the triangles on a lightly buttered baking dish and bake in a preheated oven at 300 degrees Fahrenheit for 15 to 20 minutes. Serve hot.
